name: First Cash Pawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international pawn shop operator with 3,300+ locations across the U.S., UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans, retail sales, and gold buying.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the leading international pawn shop chain with over 3,300 retail locations. The company was founded to serve cash and credit-constrained consumers through collateral-based lending and merchandise sales. FirstCash is publicly traded and included in the S&P MidCap 400 and Russell 2000 indices, employing approximately 22,000 people across the U.S., Latin America, and the United Kingdom.

FirstCash's core business model centers on pawn loans—small, non-recourse loans secured by pledged personal property—combined with retail buying and selling of merchandise. The company buys and sells jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments across its network. Beyond pawn loans, FirstCash offers layaway services (10% down payment plans), gold and precious metal buying, and retail sales. Through its subsidiary AFF, the company also provides lease-to-own and retail finance payment solutions through 15,000+ merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive geographic footprint (29 U.S. states plus D.C., UK, Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador) and scale as the leading operator in the pawn industry. The company's dual focus on lending and retail merchandise creates a comprehensive service model. Their subsidiary AFF's integration of point-of-sale payment solutions for lease-to-own arrangements expands their reach beyond traditional pawn operations into consumer finance technology.

The honest assessment is that FirstCash serves a legitimate need for immediate liquidity without credit checks, but pawn loans remain high-cost borrowing when annualized. Customers should understand that non-recourse pawn loans are fundamentally different from traditional loans—failure to repay means loss of collateral, not debt collection. The company's scale and public market status suggest operational stability, though consumers should carefully evaluate whether pawning personal items aligns with their financial situation.
address: 2648 S Parker Rd Suite 5, Aurora, CO 80014

company_info.headquarters: 2648 S Parker Rd Suite 5, Aurora, CO 80014

pros: Massive national and international footprint with 3,300+ locations for convenience and accessibility
No credit check required—serves consumers with poor or no credit history
Instant cash on the spot for pawn loans and gold/merchandise sales
Non-recourse loans mean no debt collection if you cannot repay (collateral is the only recourse)
Layaway service available with affordable 10% down payment option
Wide variety of merchandise available for purchase at discounted prices
Publicly traded company (S&P MidCap 400, Russell 2000) indicating operational transparency and stability
cons: Pawn loans are high-cost borrowing—interest rates annualize to rates significantly exceeding payday loans and personal loans
Loss of personal property if unable to repay the pawn loan within the specified timeframe
Layaway service requires upfront 10% down payment with additional fees embedded in the final purchase price
Limited by nature to small loans secured by physical collateral, not suitable for large financial needs
Merchandise quality and selection varies significantly by location; no guarantees on availability of specific items
best_for: Cash-constrained consumers with valuable personal property who need immediate liquidity without credit verification
Individuals seeking to buy discounted merchandise or jewelry without financing
Those looking for layaway options to purchase items with minimal upfront commitment
Consumers who value keeping collateral risk localized rather than facing debt collection
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for unbanked or credit-constrained consumers who own valuable personal property and need immediate cash without credit verification or formal lending processes. The critical caveat is that pawn loans are fundamentally collateral-based with high annualized costs; they work best as emergency short-term solutions, not ongoing borrowing, and require the consumer to accept losing their pledged items if unable to repay within the loan term.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property
Retail sales of jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments
Cash payment for merchandise and valuables
Retail finance payment solutions (via AFF subsidiary)
